$0062: incremented at $c106 in the NMI tail; Main_Loop at $c2e8 spins until it reaches 3, then resets it. Effectively gates the game to one logic step per 3 VBlanks (~20 Hz)
$0063: NMI prologue stash for the A register (restored by the NMI epilogue before RTI)
$0065: NMI prologue stash for the Y register
$0087: byte count consumed by PPU_WriteBlock ($801b); 58 write sites across PPU-update code
$0098: P1 controller raw state this frame (8 bits) populated by Joypad_PollOne for X=0
$0099: P1 controller newly-pressed bits this frame (rising edges of Joypad1_State)
$009a: P2 controller raw state this frame (populated by Joypad_PollOne for X=1)
$009b: P2 controller newly-pressed bits this frame
$0088: low byte of PPU destination address ($2006 target) used by PPU_WriteBlock
$0089: high byte of PPU destination address; $89:$88 is the full $2006 word
$008a: low byte of source pointer read by PPU_WriteBlock at STA $2007; zero-page ($8a),Y fetch
$008b: high byte of source pointer ($8b:$8a) used by PPU_WriteBlock
$008c: mirror of last value written to $2000; flipping bit $80 gates NMI and is reapplied across routines
$048e: entity SOA attribute 0 (active flag): bit $80 = slot in use, tested by L_f7cd. The whole entity table is 25 attributes x 25 slots, $19-byte stride per attribute, running $048e..$0682. Slot 0 is the player; Reset_Rolls clears slots 0-11 (player + co-active aux entities)
$04a7: entity SOA attribute 1 (vertical position); 25 slots at +0..+24 within the table. Slot 0 is the player: UP drives toward $56 (top), DOWN toward $c1 (bottom)
$04c0: entity SOA attribute 2 (horizontal position); 25 slots. Slot 0 is the player: LEFT drives toward $1e (left edge), RIGHT toward $e6 (right edge). L_aa61 reads Entity_X,X / Entity_X,Y for collision checks between two arbitrary slots
$03b2: shadow-OAM mirror of Entity_X[0] (player), written via DMA
$00db: frames the A button has been held; rolls over at 10 and triggers L_d3b9 (roll attempt)
$0388: bit $10 = A held this frame (debounce flag set in L_d2aa)
$0785: current-frame joypad state; A-press handler (L_d2aa) tests bit $40, the input handlers at L_d472 / L_dc54 also consume this
$0386: 0 = P1 currently playing, 1 = P2 currently playing. NewGame_Init_P1 inits to 0; SwapTo_P2 sets to 1, SwapTo_P1 / Restore_P1_NoSave set to 0
$0427: start of the 33-byte ($21) block holding the currently playing player's state. Holds Score, Lives, Rolls, Level, etc.; swapped via SwapTo_P1 / SwapTo_P2 in 2P games
$044a: 33-byte ($21) save buffer for P1 state while P2 is playing. SwapTo_P2 saves the active block here; SwapTo_P1 / Restore_P1_NoSave load it back into ActivePlayer_State
$046b: 33-byte ($21) save buffer for P2 state while P1 is playing. SwapTo_P1 saves the active block here; SwapTo_P2 loads it back into ActivePlayer_State
$03ae: nonzero -> NMI_Handler walks the PPU_UpdateQueue. NMI clears it on completion ($c07e); set by the queue builder at $81e2
$078f: variable-length PPU update queue consumed by NMI_Handler. Each entry: addr_hi, addr_lo, count, count×data_byte; queue terminates when count == 0
$07bd: first half of the warm-boot magic word; Reset_Handler skips RAM-clear when $07bd:$07be == $55:$aa
$07be: second half of the warm-boot magic word; together with WarmBoot_Magic1 distinguishes warm restart from cold power-on
$07bc: 0 = 1P game, nonzero = 2P; set in L_b6da/L_b6f7 from menu choice, gates 2P paths in Continue_Init_P1 / NewGame_Init_P2
$0385: $ff while P1 is the active player; cleared on game-over. Set at $b7da during NewGame_Init_P1
$0384: companion to P1_Active; tested by P1_LifeLost at $b80a to decide whether to swap players or game-over
$801b: copy PPU_WriteCount bytes from ($PPU_Src),Y to $2007, starting at $2006 = PPU_AddrHi:PPU_AddrLo. Called 25x; the main PPU-update primitive
$c1ad: strobe both joypads ($4016=1 then 0), then call Joypad_PollOne for X=0 (P1) and X=1 (P2). Called once per NMI from $c103 (just before VBlank_Counter is incremented)
$c1c7: shift 8 bits out of $4016,X with delta-detect: writes raw state to $98+X*2 and newly-pressed bits to $99+X*2. Bit order matches the standard A/B/SEL/START/UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT layout
$bf58: swap active player P1 -> P2: copy ActivePlayer_State to P1_SavedState, then load P2_SavedState into ActivePlayer_State, set Active_Player_Index = 1
$bf74: swap active player P2 -> P1: copy ActivePlayer_State to P2_SavedState, then load P1_SavedState into ActivePlayer_State, set Active_Player_Index = 0
$bf7f: alternate entry into SwapTo_P1: skip the save half and only load P1_SavedState into ActivePlayer_State. Used by Continue_Init_P1 because the dying P1 state is being discarded
$bf90: RESET / IRQ vector entry: SEI + CLD, set stack to $f3, enable NMI bit, wait for VBlank, then check WarmBoot_Magic. Cold path zeros all of $0000-$07ff and writes the magic; warm path skips the wipe. Both paths fall into Init_PPU_And_MainLoop
$bfca: post-Reset init: clear zero page, configure PPU ($2001 sprites on, $2000 NMI+8x16), clear scroll, fill OAM with $ff, enable APU, set up nametable pointer at $d0/$d1. Falls through to Main_Loop
$c2c0: main idle loop: spins until VBlank_Counter >= 3, resets it, re-asserts NMI enable in PPUCTRL_Shadow, then loops. Game logic runs entirely in the NMI tail; this loop just keeps the CPU alive between frames and re-arms the stack
$c030: NMI vector entry: stash A/X/Y to $63-$65, disable NMI bit in $2000, OAM-DMA the shadow OAM at $0200 via $4014, blank rendering, then if PPU_QueueReady drain PPU_UpdateQueue into $2006/$2007 and clear the flag
$a9ce: trivial SEC; RTS helper used 8x by callers that want to propagate 'failed/not-found' via carry
$b7d8: initialize P1 state at game start: clears player flags, sets Lives_Ones = $02 at $b7df (Game Genie IESUTYZA patches the immediate at $b7de). Falls through into the P1 input/play loop
$b807: P1 life-loss handler: JSR Death_Sequence, then dec Lives_Ones; on underflow borrow from Lives_Tens (set ones to 9) and re-enter at $b807. Game-over when both digits are zero
$b842: P1 continue logic in 2P mode: if TwoPlayer_Mode != 0, JSR $bf7f then reset Lives_Ones = $02 at $b84a (Game Genie IAKUUAZA patches the immediate at $b84b)
$b858: initialize P2 lives: LDA #$02 at $b858 / STA Lives_Ones at $b85a (Game Genie IASUOAZA patches the immediate at $b859). Followed by clearing the 18-byte P2 score+lives buffer at $0437..$0448
$b889: P2 life-loss handler: parallel to P1_LifeLost; same dec-with-tens-borrow pattern, with the borrow restoring Lives_Ones to $09
$d7a1: called from every life-loss path (P1_LifeLost, P2_LifeLost, etc.); resets $07c5/$0718 to $ff and clears $0439-$043c, then waits/clears state before the player respawns
$d850: set Rolls = $03 at $d852 (Game Genie PASIOALE patches the immediate at $d851), then clear $048e..$049b (12-byte block, probably entity slot)
